notes: Skill iteration 1 — first pass, single run per configuration so variance numbers represent across-eval variance not within-eval flakiness.
Without-skill in Eval 1 contradicted Vantage's actual >20% interrupt frequency, asserting interruption rates 'typically <20%' from training-data memory.
Without-skill in Eval 2 hallucinated that g6e.2xlarge has 48 GB RAM (it has 64 GB), then dismissed that instance based on the false fact.
Without-skill in Eval 3 picked db.m5.2xlarge (older generation) over the with-skill choice of db.m7i.2xlarge (current generation).
Both with-skill and without-skill in Eval 3 may have mislabeled RDS pricing columns as 'Savings Plan' — RDS uses Reserved Instances; worth verifying against the raw Vantage matrix.
With-skill runs averaged ~+50s and ~+34k tokens. The skill is heavier upfront but produces more rigorous outputs.
